item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of SCP-5000, in that the Foundation goes to war, using anomalies…

btw, is the Asian history basically the same? I wonder how these things affected them. But Korea must've split early, and if China joins Soviet Russia…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The amount of deviations from the original

tsk tsk. you mean number of deviations? 🙄

Also:

Following this incident, the entire county of [REDACTED] was given Class-A amnestics over the course of five days, the elimination of all recordings of SCP-2238-A-23, and the detainment of over ████ staff members involved in the film.

This… sentence doesn't line up properly

item.title: Re: UPDATE
item.content:encoded: Minor thing I found, in the text it says there is an interview with O5-12, while it is O5-14 quoted in the excerpt.

item.title: UPDATE
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so a wiser and more knowledgeable BananaRepublic looked over the timeline for this and thought "oh boy this sucks dick".

Thankfully, I've attempted a rewrite of the timeline to be more accurate.

Any other criticisms are appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minor nitpick: they don't use "County" in Louisiana. They use "Parish," (St. Martin Parish, Terrebonne Parish, [REDACTED] Parish)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm disappointed that GAW gets a mention despite being a part of a GoI that would be irrelevant in a war between the Foundation and the GOC, yet GoIs that would have reasonably been involved in some way, or at least had a leg to stand or a something to gain or lose and some way of attempting to influence the outcome in such a conflict are not mentioned like the Chaos Insurgency, CoTBG, any of the Sarkic Cults, UIU, or even reletivly small and self contained groups like the one in SCP-1659.

item.title: Image Credit
item.content:encoded:
The picture used here is a modified version of this picture here taken in 1943 by the US army, and as such is within the public domain.

Additionally, user Cyantreuse1 modified this picture.

Footnotes
1. A million thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's Trotsky

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoever made the page image needs to touch it up a bit. Gandhi obviously looks photoshopped in and lacks the grain and off-focus that Roosevelt and Churchill have. Also, it's better to save such images as JPEGs instead of PNGs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, so, since the ending of the contest, I have removed the reference to SCP-096, and am considering more rewrites later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird. SCPper (chrome extension) says that it was translated by BananaRepublic from the 'original', which just links to a page that says 'undefined'. This also seems to be a problem here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…god damn, that last extract broke my heart.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the premise of the war is really quite silly.

A lot of real world conflicts are that way, really.

Ever heard of the War of the Bucket?
A stolen bucket turned a medieval cold war hot, leading directly to a retaliatory battle with over 2000 casualties.

The US and Britain almost went to war when a guy shot another guy's pig because it kept getting into his garden. The problem: they lived on one of those "no, I'm pretty sure we own it." border islands. Just to clarify, this was not a minor border dispute. Forts were built. Naval fleets were dispatched. Kaiser Wilhelm the 1st oversaw a year-long negotiation to end a twelve-year standoff over this.

Probably the best example, though: You know how they always point to the assassination of Archduke Ferdinand as the start of WWI? Funny thing: he was kind of a dick, and the general response in Austria was "…and we care why?". He did, however, make a good excuse for Austria to have some anti-Serbian riots and make some outrageous reparation demands from Serbia. Turned out that wasn't such a great idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not a giant fan of the bottom half of the article - while I can understand a potential 096 breach and the subsequent amnestizing of an entire county, I'm not a giant fan of how casual the Foundation-GOC conflict is presented: it seems really randomly strewn, and present really no continuity or logic other than just "Foundation stuff because Foundation".

The interviews are fine and convincing, but the premise of the war is really quite silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: And the Japanese wiki already has a translation of this article despite not being a week old. I'm…..I'm so fucking shocked.

The Japanese site is a rather prolific lot, I would say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I seem to have an issue with this article. Half the time when I view it, there isn't an image; the other half there is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really know much about translations, but I know if you ALREADY have one your article is probably better than average, even without the kinda cringey 096 breach.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this concept for hiscon, an an alternate reality being aired on TV. The other thing that makes it shocking is that it knew about the foundation and its tracking of it. I do second other criticisms such as the high number of 096 deaths.

In the end however, your pros outweigh the cons

Take my +1

PS: I loved your nod to dr mann and the broken masquerade canon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These largely cover my feelings. The 096 reference was gooofy and immersion-breaking; anything more than four figures is just silly. (I'm with MacLeod on the crop-dusters though.)

The alternate history was alright, if nothing fantastical, and there were some off elements (did you mean Czarina Alexandra? Also, Napoleonic France degrading Indio a dark age… could use some more explanation, let's say.) The GOC/Foundation war had a fair amount of war crime/tragedy to it, but nothing I haven't seen more convincingly expressed elsewhere, and the events expressed there aren't explored in much detail, just kind of sprinkled in.

No vote overall, I think. The 096 reference just knocked me right out of it, and the rest sort of floats just near the level of being really interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this the alt-reality from the first chapter of War is Child's Play?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: County, not country. Couple dozen crop dusters could do it if your headcanon allows for that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have heard the criticism, and I can see where it comes from. I can cut some of the things that feel forced and/or stupid, but only after the contest due to the parameters of said contest. I'll cut out 096 and some other ones.

Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love, love, love the topic of alternate history, so I had high hopes for this article coming in, but there were just a bunch of things that didn't quite make sense to me (similarily to what TyGently and Cyantreuse both mentioned) that made it hard for me to stomach, such as:

  • Somehow most of central/southern/eastern Europe experiences little change-which doesn't seem to make sense if Napoleon conquered their governments and replaced them. Even if they were conquered, things like Italy's unification and the formation of Poland were highly highly dependent on events in Austria and Germany that presumably would have never happened in this timeline.
  • France goes from "collapsing" to somehow being in control over most of Europe with no explanation, which is inconsistent.
  • For some reason France invades Great Britain and the Soviet Union for oil of all things, which makes little to no sense.

Things like this really hurt the immersion of the article, and the randomness of 096 and a bunch of other skips being tossed in for what seems like name dropping reasons kind of kills it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Inclusion of 096 is a bit whack. Killing six-figure numbers of civilians is patently ridiculous, as well as off-handedly amnesticizing an entire country in five days.

Seconding this. I had to check to see if there was a major hurricane or something during that period, but there doesn't seem to have been one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Although I second TyGently about some stuff, this had enough effort and historical context put into it that I consider it upvote-worthy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one's a very mixed bag, so I'm novoting for now. There were quite a few spelling errors and minor formatting issues, which I did my best to fix, feel free to roll it back if I made any changes in error.

Showing an alt-history can be fruitful, and you do some good things with it, but the migration from "alt-history" to "Foundation-GOC war" doesn't feel narratively natural, and the flashpoint being "whoops the Foundation accidentally killed three thousand gock agents" doesn't make a ton of sense.

Notable Good Bits:

  • The picture is nice, compliments to Cyan.
  • The original timeline does paint a pretty interesting image of the world.
  • The Excerpts at the end are, in general, pretty well-written, and avoid narm for the most part. My favorite was the testimony from the Brazil resident.
  • The conclusion is relatively strong.

Notable Bad Bits:

  • Inclusion of 096 is a bit whack. Killing six-figure numbers of civilians is patently ridiculous, as well as off-handedly amnesticizing an entire country in five days.
  • Detaining over a thousand staff members over something obviously not from our timeline does not reflect well on the Foundation. It is very hard for a piece to be convincing when it makes the Foundation look incompetent.
  • The lack of information on what happens post-1946 or post-occult-war is a bit frustrating. Surely the Foundation could infer from the narration at least a few things about what the present is like in this world? For example, "unlike x today…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have worked with something that was roughly standard and made something meaningful out of it.
Hyped to see this worked out. Welcome to the having-a-mainlist club, I'll surely be joining you soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I was very nervous when I first posted it, feeling that it would have failed, being my first SCP. Thank you for the upvote and creating this contest, which allowed me to write this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thoughts
hide

This is pretty cool, I agree with Conwell below that anomalous broadcasts aren't the most original framing device in the world but the documents more than make up for it. There's an enticing timeline here and getting into an alternate universe unveiled Foundation is a great way to escalate things to a conclusion. I noticed a few SCP-XXXX's here and there but other than that, well done.

The picture is also pretty nifty, I wish there were more of them in the article but the one you've got does its job well enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well alright. I like this.

A fake documentary series is not the most groundbreaking way of showing an alt-history, but you more than make up for that, in my opinion, by having two very interesting timelines at work. The excepts at the end were a particularly enjoyable part of the article.

I'd be interested in learning how the other GOIs fit into the war. Did MC&D, the Factory, and Anderson Robotics make a killing selling items to both sides? Did MC attempt humanitarian aid to those caught in the war zones? What was AWCY doing during all these shenanigans?

Overall, a solid skip for the contest. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to all the people who looked over this thing. I really do. I don't have much of a response at the moment.

Edit: Wow, I didn't expect this to do that well. I was panicking and wondering if this was even gonna stick for more than a day, but, wow. Now that the picture is is up, I have to give thanks to Cyantreuse , who made the picture. Thanks to Ihp, AbsentMindedNihlist, Shio, Lazar, and so many many others for looking over this. I never thought I would have made it on the mainlist, or on the site. Thank you. Thank you all.

Edit: And the Japanese wiki already has a translation of this article despite not being a week old. I'm…..I'm so fucking shocked.

Edit: Its….its at +40. Thank you, thank you all. I never expected this. Thank you all.
